gameSubtitle: Drop each tile using the two-condition rule: Has mass AND takes up space
definition: Matter = has mass + takes up space

matter.label: Matter
matter.description: Has mass AND takes up space

notMatter.label: Not Matter
notMatter.description: Missing mass OR takes no space

tiles.label: Water
tiles.correctReason: Water has mass and takes up space (it fills a container) → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Think: can you pour water into a glass? Does it fill that space? Does it have weight?

tiles.label: Stone
tiles.correctReason: Stone has measurable mass and clearly occupies space → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Can you hold a stone? Does it weigh something? Does it take up room on a table?

tiles.label: Air
tiles.correctReason: Air has mass (a filled balloon weighs more than empty one) and takes up space → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Tricky! Inflate a balloon — air fills it (takes space) and adds weight (has mass). Check both conditions!

tiles.label: Sunlight
tiles.correctReason: Sunlight is electromagnetic radiation — it has no rest mass and takes no space → Not Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Apply the two conditions: does sunlight have mass? Can you put sunlight in a box and close the lid?

tiles.label: A Dream
tiles.correctReason: Dreams are mental experiences — no mass, no physical space occupied → Not Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Does a dream weigh anything? Can you put a dream in a container? Check both conditions!

tiles.label: Wood
tiles.correctReason: Wood has mass and occupies space — a classic solid example of matter → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Can you pick up a piece of wood? Does it weigh something? Does it take up space on a shelf?

tiles.label: Milk
tiles.correctReason: Milk is a liquid with mass that fills and takes up space in a container → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Pour some milk — does it fill the glass (take space)? Does a full glass weigh more than an empty one (mass)?

tiles.label: Sound
tiles.correctReason: Sound is a wave of energy (vibrations) — no mass, no space occupied → Not Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Sound travels through matter but isn't matter itself. Can you weigh sound? Can you put it in a box?

tiles.label: Fire Flame
tiles.correctReason: A flame is a process — plasma light and heat energy, not a substance with rest mass → Not Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Edge case! The gases burning are matter, but the flame (light + heat) itself has no mass. What are the two conditions?

tiles.label: Shadow
tiles.correctReason: A shadow is the absence of light — it has no mass and takes no physical space → Not Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Can you weigh a shadow? Can you scoop a shadow into a jar? It has no mass and no physical presence.

tiles.label: Honey
tiles.correctReason: Honey is a thick liquid with mass that flows and occupies space → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Honey is a liquid — does it have weight? Does it fill a jar? Apply the two-condition rule!

tiles.label: Oxygen
tiles.correctReason: Oxygen gas has mass and takes up space (like all gases) → Matter ✓
tiles.wrongHint: Like air, oxygen is a gas. Does it have mass? A tank of oxygen weighs more when full. Does it take up space?
